mechanism_narrative: An elevated unemployment rate depresses household income and consumption expenditures, transmitting downward pressure on corporate sales expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 7%, or if the rank correlation between UNRATE and 20-day forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, the zero lower bound eliminates near-term tightening risk and anchors short-term discount rates, compressing the equity risk premium and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or if the average 5-day return during such periods does not exceed the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal a coordinated recovery in output and labor demand, raising expected aggregate income and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered months, or if the interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS changes fails to achieve a positive and significant coefficient (p < 0.05) in a regression over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when unemployment exceeds 7%, a month-over-month rise in retail sales indicates that household spending is resisting labor-market headwinds, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE > 7% and RSAFS rises month-over-month, or if the predictive power of RSAFS (rank IC) is below 0.03 in the UNRATE > 7% subsample over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Depressed industrial production reflects underutilized manufacturing capacity and weak goods demand, which erodes corporate operating leverage and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when INDPRO is below 100, or if the rank correlation between INDPRO and 20-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 900 thousand units signal a shrinking residential construction sector, which reduces demand for building materials, consumer durables, and mortgage credit, transmitting negative demand shocks to the broader economy and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when HOUST is below 900, or if the average 20-day return during triggered periods is not significantly below the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The coexistence of unemployment above 7% and a federal funds rate below 0.25% indicates a liquidity trap where conventional monetary stimulus is exhausted and ineffective at reducing labor market slack, causing investors to mark down long-term earnings growth and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS fails to show a negative and significant coefficient (p < 0.05) in a regression over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Subpar real GDP reflects deficient aggregate demand and idle economic capacity, which constrains corporate revenue growth and profit margins across sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 is below 14000, or if the rank correlation between GDPC1 and 60-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.
